summary: Build (tsc --noEmit) and tests (115/115) pass, but there are critical runtime data loss bugs in goal-repo.ts where SQL binding parameter names (@drive_thread_id, @thread_id) no longer match the object keys (drive_channel_id, channel_id) after the rename. This causes goalChannelId and task channelId to be saved as NULL and read back as empty/undefined. Additionally, the orchestrator notify() method and replanner.ts still use threadId parameter names, and several comments reference the old terminology.

issues.description: SQL binding mismatch: upsertGoal SQL uses @drive_thread_id (lines 42,47,55) but goalDriveStateToGoalRow() returns { drive_channel_id: ... } (line 191). better-sqlite3 binds by key name, so @drive_thread_id gets undefined → NULL. Goal's goalChannelId is silently lost on every save. Fix: either change SQL to @drive_channel_id or change the row object key to drive_thread_id.

SQL binding mismatch: INSERT INTO goal_tasks uses @thread_id (line 112) but the run() object uses key 'channel_id' (line 133). Task channelId is silently saved as NULL through GoalRepo.save() path (called by orchestrator saveState ~39 times). Fix: change SQL binding to @channel_id or change object key to thread_id.

GoalRow.drive_channel_id doesn't match the actual DB column name 'drive_thread_id' (migration 001, never renamed). SELECT * returns column 'drive_thread_id', so GoalRow.drive_channel_id will always be undefined when reading from DB. Either rename the DB column via migration or keep the TypeScript property as drive_thread_id.
notify() method parameter still named 'threadId' (line 2807). Internal references at lines 2825, 2832, 2837 also use 'threadId'. Should be renamed to 'channelId' for consistency.
HandleReplanDeps.notify callback parameter still named 'threadId'. Should be 'channelId' for consistency.
handleReplanByImpact callback uses 'threadId' variable name: (threadId, message, type, options) => this.notify(threadId, ...). Should use channelId.
Comment says 'refix 只对 failed 且有 threadId 的任务有效' — should say channelId.
Comment says '保留 branchName, threadId, dispatchedAt' — should say channelId.
JSDoc comment says '通过 guildId/threadId 发送消息' — should say channelId.
File header comment still says ':threadId'. Should say ':channelId'.
File header comments (lines 2-5) still reference ':threadId'. Should say ':channelId'.
File header comments (lines 6-10) still reference ':threadId'. Should say ':channelId'.
File header comment still says ':threadId'. Should say ':channelId'.
Log message still says 'Background chat started for thread' instead of 'channel'. Same at lines 20, 22, 23.
